title: The Best New Bars in Miami
summary: As a gateway between the U.S., the Caribbean, and Latin America, Miami has long been a champion of diverse flavors — rum and agave spirits mingling with notes of citrus and exotic fruits. Recently, vermouth and sherry-based drinks, along with classic Spanish cocktails and…
whatHappening: Miami's bar scene is experiencing a notable shift toward vermouth and sherry-based cocktails, moving beyond the rum and agave spirits that have historically dominated the market. New establishments are embracing classic Spanish cocktail traditions, reflecting evolving consumer tastes and mixology trends.
whoInvolved: Eater Miami, the influential food and drink publication covering South Florida's restaurant and bar industry, is documenting these emerging venues and industry shifts through their coverage.
whyMatters: The evolution signals changing preferences among Miami's sophisticated drinkers and positions the city's bar culture at the forefront of national cocktail trends. This diversification strengthens Miami's reputation as a dynamic culinary destination and creates business opportunities for hospitality entrepreneurs capitalizing on emerging spirits categories.
moreContext: Miami's geographic position as a cultural crossroads between the U.S., Caribbean, and Latin America has historically made it a laboratory for fusion flavors and international spirits. The traditional dominance of rum and agave-based drinks reflected this heritage and accessibility. The recent pivot toward European fortified wines like vermouth and sherry suggests Miami's bar community is maturing and exploring more sophisticated, Old World cocktail traditions—a trend consistent with broader American bartending movements favoring classical preparation methods and heritage spirits over trend-driven novelty.

This approach reflects broader media trends toward weekly digest formats that aggregate local coverage for busy audiences.

title: Eating News in Miami, July 2026
summary: A different kind of tea time -- tipsy tea, an unbeatable offer for mango lovers, new high profile restaurant in Coral Gables, ladies night with all the perks, a new waterfront option for cocktails and delicious comfort food from leading chefs, decadent ice cream and churros,…
whatHappening: Miami's food and beverage scene is buzzing with new summer offerings in July 2026, from tipsy tea experiences and mango-focused promotions to ladies nights and waterfront cocktail bars. Multiple high-profile restaurants are launching or expanding with chef-driven menus featuring comfort food, gourmet ice cream, and churros.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, the local food and lifestyle publication, is covering a mix of established chefs and new restaurant ventures opening across Miami neighborhoods including Coral Gables. The outlets range from casual comfort food establishments to upscale waterfront dining destinations.
whyMatters: These dining developments signal strong consumer spending and hospitality growth in Miami's competitive restaurant market during the summer season. The variety of offerings—from budget-friendly ladies nights to premium waterfront experiences—demonstrates how restaurants are targeting diverse demographics to drive traffic during traditionally slower summer months.
moreContext: Miami's food scene has intensified competition post-pandemic, with establishments increasingly relying on themed promotions and experiential dining to differentiate themselves. Waterfront dining continues to be a premium category in Miami, capitalizing on the city's geographic assets. title: From Casual Lunch to Serious Dinner: Paperfish Sushi Brickell Stepped Up Its Game
summary: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has always been one of my “go to” places to have lunch or happy hour with a friend and they always like it. With all of the sushi restaurants in town you’d ask why Paperfish Sushi? The lovely décor that brightens up your day the moment you walk in,…
whatHappening: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has elevated its restaurant concept from a casual lunch and happy hour destination to a serious dinner establishment. The Miami sushi spot is expanding its appeal beyond its traditional daytime clientele.
whoInvolved: Paperfish Sushi Brickell is a sushi restaurant located in Miami's Brickell neighborhood that has built a loyal following as a casual dining spot. The establishment is reviewed by Miami Curated, a local lifestyle publication covering dining and entertainment in the Miami area.
whyMatters: The upgrade positions Paperfish to compete in Miami's competitive fine dining sushi market and capture higher-value dinner service revenue. This move allows the restaurant to serve customers looking for more upscale experiences while retaining its existing lunch crowd.
moreContext: Paperfish Sushi has maintained a loyal customer base through its distinctive décor and consistent quality, making it a go-to spot for casual business lunches and after-work drinks. Miami's Brickell neighborhood hosts numerous sushi restaurants, creating intense competition that requires differentiation. The restaurant's strategy of upgrading its dinner service while maintaining its casual lunch identity reflects a broader trend of restaurants expanding their daypart offerings to maximize revenue. This evolution suggests Paperfish is responding to customer demand and market opportunity to position itself as a more versatile dining destination.

title: New Places to Shop, Eat in the Miami Design District
summary: With the crowds having gone from the Dolce & Gabbana show and many from FIFA, now is a good time to make a visit to the Miami Design District, especially since new shops have opened that are brands that are probably new to you as well as restaurants. Plus, you might come across…

title: PASTA: The Model Miami Restaurant
summary: Very reasonable prices, excellent food across the board, very good service, low noise decibel so you can easily talk, PASTA is a dream restaurant which should serve as a model to others in Miami. I'll even go so far as to say it has the best pasta in Miami. And on top of all…
whatHappening: PASTA, a Miami restaurant, is being highlighted as an exemplary dining establishment that excels across multiple dimensions. The venue is earning praise for delivering excellent food, particularly pasta dishes, at reasonable prices with attentive service.
whoInvolved: PASTA is a Miami-based restaurant that has caught the attention of Miami Curated, a local dining and lifestyle publication. The restaurant operates as a standalone establishment setting standards for service quality and value in Miami's competitive dining scene.
whyMatters: PASTA's success demonstrates that restaurants can deliver premium dining experiences without excessive pricing or pretension, potentially influencing how other Miami establishments operate. The recognition positions the restaurant as a model for combining affordability, quality cuisine, and hospitality in a market often dominated by high-end venues.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant scene is known for experiential dining and upscale establishments, making PASTA's emphasis on reasonable pricing and accessible excellence a notable differentiator. The restaurant prioritizes practical dining amenities like low noise levels that facilitate conversation—a feature often overlooked in trendier venues. The endorsement suggests a growing appetite among diners for unpretentious, quality-focused restaurants that prioritize fundamentals: food, service, and value. This review reflects broader consumer trends favoring substance over atmosphere in post-pandemic dining preferences.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour in Miami Beach is emerging as a premier destination that combines upscale living with local authenticity. The neighborhood is positioning itself as an alternative to the tourist-heavy Ocean Drive, offering a more refined and wellness-oriented lifestyle on the bay side of South Beach.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is highlighting this neighborhood trend through local journalism and curation. Sunset Harbour's appeal centers on its local residents, boutique businesses, and wellness-focused establishments that define its character.
whyMatters: This shift reflects broader Miami Beach real estate and lifestyle trends as affluent residents and tourists seek alternatives to overcrowded party districts. The neighborhood's success could reshape how Miami Beach markets itself beyond its party reputation, attracting a demographic prioritizing quality of life and local experiences.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour sits on the bay side of South Beach, geographically separated from the frenetic Ocean Drive corridor. The neighborhood has traditionally been quieter than its beachfront counterpart but has gained prominence as Miami's lifestyle market matures. This reflects a larger U.S. urban trend where walkability, wellness amenities, and local food scenes drive neighborhood desirability. Miami's evolution from a party destination to a sophisticated residential hub has accelerated post-pandemic as remote workers and entrepreneurs relocated to South Florida.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant month, is returning this July with 75 participating restaurants. The lineup represents the event's largest roster to date, featuring everything from Michelin-recognized fine dining to casual neighborhood spots across diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with its local restaurant community. The participating venues range from upscale Michelin-recognized establishments to everyday neighborhood favorites, representing the full spectrum of the city's dining scene.
whyMatters: The expanded restaurant month drives foot traffic and revenue to Coral Gables' dining district during a typically slower summer period. For diners, it offers an accessible way to sample premium restaurants at special promotional prices, potentially building long-term customer loyalty.
moreContext: Taste the Gables is an annual culinary event designed to spotlight the city's restaurant scene and attract both local and regional diners. Restaurant months have become a popular marketing tool for dining destinations nationwide, with similar programs running in major cities. The inclusion of 75 venues signals strong economic health in Coral Gables' hospitality sector and suggests restaurants see value in participating despite summer's slower season. July timing allows the city to counter typical summer business dips when residents may travel or dine out less frequently.
